evidence.title: Chemistry: Atoms First 2e
evidence.locator: Occurrence, Preparation, and Compounds of Oxygen
evidence.usage: Representative normal oxide, peroxide, superoxide, and covalent oxide outcomes

evidence.title: Chemistry 2e
evidence.usage: Charge neutrality, electron transfer, fixed-charge monatomic ions, and binary ionic formula units

evidence.title: NIST Chemistry WebBook
evidence.publisher: National Institute of Standards and Technology
evidence.locator: Standard state phase data for the recorded species
evidence.usage: Ambient standard-state phases for oxygen-family reactants and oxides

premises.statement: The listed element families have the representative balanced oxygen outcomes encoded by their supported cases.
Oxygen and oxide products use explicit localized or delocalized bonds, formal charges, ionic components, and representative network fragments.
The listed electron states are the closed valence domain used by the oxygen reaction operations.
Formation of the oxide product and disappearance of the reactant are compatible generic observations for these representative theoretical experiences.
Fixed-charge binary ionic formula units use the smallest whole-number cation-to-anion ratio whose component charges sum to zero.
A binary ionic formula unit is represented by explicitly charged monatomic components in a charge-aware ionic association.
The fixed-charge ion-pair domain transfers the cation charge to anion valence vacancies after explanatory elemental-bond cleavage.
Formation of the binary salt and disappearance of the selected elemental reactants are compatible generic theoretical observations.
Each recorded oxygen-family species is presented in its ambient standard-state phase: gases O2, H2, CO2, and SO2; liquids H2O and Mn2O7; all other recorded elemental subjects and oxides as solids.
